serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 200 ml can of San Pellegrino Pompelmo, as served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk and other Italian restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, grape drink — adapted to San Pellegrino Pompelmo product label data; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central NDB 14148 (carbonated beverage, fruit-flavoured, lower calorie) and Sanpellegrino official product nutritional declaration
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) cross-referenced with Sanpellegrino product label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: San Pellegrino Pompelmo is a branded Italian sparkling grapefruit beverage. Nutrient values are derived from the official Sanpellegrino product nutritional declaration (per 100 ml): energy 36 kcal, carbohydrate 8.8 g (of which sugars 8.8 g), protein 0 g, fat 0 g, fibre 0 g, sodium 0.01 g (10 mg). These values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for carbonated fruit-flavoured beverages. Per-100g values are treated as equivalent to per-100ml given the near-water density of this beverage.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fruit juice and fruit-based beverages; grapefruit juice, unsweetened (GI ~48); sugar-sweetened carbonated fruit beverage analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for San Pellegrino Pompelmo in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 52 based on analogous sugar-sweetened carbonated grapefruit/citrus beverages. Grapefruit juice (unsweetened) has a published GI of approximately 48 (Atkinson et al., 2021). The addition of sugar in Pompelmo raises the effective GI slightly; an estimate of 52 is applied, consistent with low-GI sugar-sweetened fruit beverages in the Sydney database. Glycemic load per 200 ml serving = (52 × 17.6 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 9.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (10 mg per 100 ml; 20 mg per 200 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: San Pellegrino Pompelmo is very low in sodium (approximately 20 mg per 200 ml serving), making it acceptable for individuals managing hypertension, though the added sugar content should be considered in the context of overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (8.8 g per 100 ml; 17.6 g per 200 ml serving) and moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 200 ml serving of San Pellegrino Pompelmo delivers approximately 17.6 g of sugar and a glycemic load of 9.2, which can contribute to postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption and account for this beverage within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, and low sodium; moderate free sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: San Pellegrino Pompelmo contains no fat or saturated fat and is very low in sodium, which is favourable for cardiovascular health; however, the free sugar content (17.6 g per 200 ml serving) should be moderated in line with WHO guidelines recommending less than 25 g of free sugars per day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: San Pellegrino Pompelmo is a commercially produced Italian sparkling grapefruit beverage manufactured by Sanpellegrino S.p.A., a subsidiary of Nestlé. It is sold in a distinctive 200 ml can and is widely recognised as a premium Italian soft drink. The beverage is made with carbonated water, grapefruit juice, sugar, citric acid, and natural grapefruit flavouring, giving it a lightly sweet, pleasantly bitter citrus flavour with fine effervescence. It is served chilled and is popular as a table beverage in Italian restaurants worldwide, including in Singapore where it is offered at establishments such as Ricciotti - The Riverwalk. Nutritionally, each 200 ml serving provides approximately 72 kcal, 17.6 g of sugar, negligible protein and fat, and about 20 mg of sodium. The beverage has an estimated glycemic index of 52 (low GI category) and a glycemic load of approximately 9.2 per serving. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is gluten-free and dairy-free. Clinically, the beverage is low in sodium and fat, making it broadly acceptable for most dietary patterns, but its free sugar content warrants moderation for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or following low-sugar dietary regimens. The Italian word 'pompelmo' means grapefruit, and the product is part of the Sanpellegrino Sparkling Fruit Beverages range, which also includes flavours such as Aranciata (orange), Limonata (lemon), and Aranciata Rossa (blood orange).

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from the official Sanpellegrino product nutritional declaration cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central data for analogous carbonated fruit beverages. The product label provides reliable macro data. GI is an analogous estimate based on published values for similar sugar-sweetened citrus beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the approved sources.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples' to 'Beverages', which is the correct classification for a sparkling fruit drink. 2. Serving size confirmed as 200 ml per the input description and standard Sanpellegrino can size. 3. protein_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Sanpellegrino product label; this beverage contains no protein by formulation. 4. fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Sanpellegrino product label; this beverage contains no fat by formulation. 5. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label; no dietary fibre present in this carbonated beverage. 6. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-derived carbonated beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 — naturally zero as fat_g is 0.0. 8. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported on the Sanpellegrino product label or in the cross-referenced USDA source for this specific product. 9. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 10. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 11. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not quantified in the approved source despite grapefruit juice being present; the processing and dilution level make the contribution uncertain. 12. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are all -1 as they are not reported in the source database for this product. 13. glycemic_index set to 52 (low GI category, ≤55) as an analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened citrus/grapefruit beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this branded product. 14. Glycemic load per 200 ml serving calculated as (52 × 17.6) / 100 = 9.15, rounded to 9.2. 15. Multilingual local_names are null as this is an Italian branded commercial product with no established local-language names in the Singaporean context; the product name 'Pompelmo' is Italian and is captured in romanized_local and aliases. 16. The input cuisine_tags ('Pasta', 'Pizza', 'Chicken Dish') reflect the restaurant category rather than the beverage itself and are not carried forward as food-level attributes.
